factors.summary: Order backlog is not explicitly mentioned, but new orders are outpacing revenue growth.

evidence.text: Net sales were $777.8 for the three months ended February 29, 2016 compared with $616.1 reported for the three months ended February 28, 2015, an increase of $161.7, or 26.2%.

factors.summary: The company is holding prices, with a 1% unfavorable price/mix impact, indicating some pricing pressure but overall stability.

evidence.text: The change in price/mix was due primarily to unfavorable pricing on LED luminaires, reflecting the decline in certain LED component costs, as well as a change in sales channel mix.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of Fiscal 2016 Compared with Second Quarter of Fiscal 2015

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin LED products, which are beneficial for the company.

evidence.text: Sales of LED-based products increased approximately 40% compared to the year-ago period and represented approximately 55% of total net sales.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of Fiscal 2016 Compared with Second Quarter of Fiscal 2015

factors.summary: Component costs are declining, aiding gross margin expansion.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin increased 180 basis points to 43.3% for the three months ended February 29, 2016 compared with 41.5% in the prior-year period. Gross profit was higher than the prior-year period due primarily to additional contribution on higher net sales, productivity improvements, as well as lower material and component costs and lower warranty and recall costs.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of Fiscal 2016 Compared with Second Quarter of Fiscal 2015

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, driven by higher employee-related expenses and share-based compensation.

evidence.text: The increase in SD&A expenses was due primarily to higher costs to support the greater sales volume, including freight and commissions, higher employee-related costs, including share-based incentive compensation, increased SD&A expense related to acquired businesses, and increased amortization expense of acquired intangible assets.

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives are partially offsetting higher costs, but there's no explicit mention of measurable savings.

factors.summary: The company is focused on expanding its lighting solutions, particularly in smart buildings and IoT, indicating a clear long-term strategy.

evidence.text: The transition to solid-state lighting provides the opportunity for lighting to be integrated with other building automation systems to create an optimal platform for enabling the “Internet of Things” (IoT), which will support the advancement of smart buildings, smart cities, and the smart grid.

factors.summary: The company is sensitive to commodity prices, particularly LED component costs, and foreign currency rate changes.

evidence.text: The change in price/mix was due primarily to unfavorable pricing on LED luminaires, reflecting the decline in certain LED component costs, as well as a change in sales channel mix. Due to the changing dynamics of the Company's product portfolio, including the increase of integrated lighting solutions as well as the proliferation of new products due to the adoption of solid-state lighting, it is not possible to precisely quantify volume or differentiate the individual components of price/mix.

factors.summary: Recent acquisitions and investments in smart lighting solutions are catalysts for future growth.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is stable, with total debt slightly increasing but within manageable levels.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is positive, supporting operations and growth initiatives.

evidence.text: The Company generated $119.5 of cash flow from operating activities during the six months ended February 29, 2016 compared with $75.5 in the prior-year period, an increase of $44.0, due primarily to higher net income and lower operating working capital requirements partially offset by increased variable incentive compensation payments.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, contributing to revenue growth, particularly in LED products.

evidence.text: Sales volume was higher across most product categories and key sales channels as the Company realized greater demand for LED-based luminaires. Sales of LED-based luminaires during the first six months of fiscal 2016 increased approximately 40% compared to the year-ago period.

evidence.text: Sales volume was higher across most product categories and key sales channels as the Company realized greater demand for LED-based luminaires.

factors.summary: The North American lighting market is growing, driven by new construction and renovation activity, with the fastest growth in LED lighting and integrated solutions.

evidence.text: The growth rate for the North American lighting market, which typically benefits from new construction as well as renovation and retrofit activity, is projected to be in the mid-to-upper single digit range for fiscal 2016 with continued growth over the next several years.

evidence.text: The Company is one of the world’s leading providers of lighting and energy management solutions for commercial, institutional, industrial, infrastructure, and residential applications throughout North America and select international markets.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is strong and accelerating, driven by increased sales volume and market expansion. The growth is supported by both higher sales volumes and increased product demand.

evidence.text: Net sales were $1,514.4 for the six months ended February 29, 2016 compared with $1,263.5 reported for the six months ended February 28, 2015, an increase of $250.9, or 19.9%. Sales of LED-based luminaires during the first six months of fiscal 2016 increased approximately 40% compared to the year-ago period.

factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating, particularly driven by increased sales of LED-based luminaires, which saw a significant increase of approximately 40% in both the first six months of fiscal 2016 and the second quarter of fiscal 2016 compared to the respective prior periods.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins have shown consistent expansion over the first six months and the second quarter of fiscal 2016 compared to the respective prior-year periods. The improvements are attributed to higher gross profits, with some offset by increased costs in the second quarter.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin increased to 43.3% for the six months ended February 29, 2016 compared with 41.8% in the prior-year period. Operating profit for the first six months of fiscal 2016 was $219.1 compared with $165.3 for the prior-year period, an increase of $53.8, or 32.5%.
evidence.subsection: First Six Months of Fiscal 2016 Compared with First Six Months of Fiscal 2015

evidence.text: Gross profit margin increased 180 basis points to 43.3% for the three months ended February 29, 2016 compared with 41.5% in the prior-year period. Operating profit increased 35.8% to $106.7 compared with $78.6 for the prior-year period.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of Fiscal 2016 Compared with Second Quarter of Fiscal 2015

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated significant merger and acquisition activity, with several acquisitions made to expand product offerings and capabilities. These include the acquisition of Distech Controls, Juno Lighting, and Geometri, which are expected to be accretive to financial results.

evidence.text: The Company completed the acquisition of Distech Controls, a provider of building automation solutions. Additionally, the Company acquired Juno Lighting and Geometri in mid-December 2015. Management expects these acquisitions to be accretive to the Company's fiscal 2016 consolidated financial results.
